topic.name: Government Accountability and Efficiency
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing accountability, transparency, and efficiency in federal government operations and programs.

Document 1: Requires the head of each executive agency to submit to the President and Congress a report on the implementation of recommendations made by specified Government Accountability Office (GAO) reports on reducing duplication in government programs.
Agency heads to begin implementation of such recommendations in order to eliminate, consolidate, streamline, or better coordinate government programs.
Document 2: Establishes the Federal Agency Sunset Commission to review and evaluate the efficiency and public need for each agency.
Requires the abolishment of any agency within one year of the Commission's review, unless the agency is reauthorized by Congress.
Document 3: Requires each person, state, local, or tribal government, or any government corporation that receives appropriated funds to report at least once quarterly each receipt and use of such funds.
Directs the Commission to require continuous or automatic reporting to the extent practicable.
Document 4: Requires the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to coordinate with the heads of federal agencies to determine costs associated with the purchase or lease of civilian and non-tactical vehicles.
Directs the Inspector General of each federal agency to review the system for monitoring the use of motor vehicles owned or leased by such agency for non-official use.
Document 5: Requires the inspectors general of federal agencies to supervise, direct, and control audit and investigative activities pertaining to programs and operations.

Document 6: Establishes the Office of Inspector General for the Judicial Branch of the U.S. government to conduct investigations of alleged misconduct in the Judicial Branch.
Conduct and supervise audits and investigations to prevent and detect waste, fraud, and abuse.
Document 7: Requires the Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to establish and maintain a single website accessible by the public for obtaining electronic copies of congressionally mandated reports.
Document 8: Requires the Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to issue guidance to prohibit the download, installation, or use by government employees and contractors of open-network peer-to-peer file sharing software.

topic.name: Offshore Oil and Gas Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and regulations governing the exploration, leasing, and production of oil and gas resources in offshore areas, particularly the Outer Continental Shelf.

Document 1: Declares without force or effect all federal prohibitions against the expenditure of appropriated funds to conduct natural gas leasing and preleasing activities for any area of the Outer Continental Shelf (OCS).

waive the adjusted gross income limitation applicable to the child tax credit for such military families.
